transitions: Ready for Dev â†’ In Development
External Review â†’ Ready for QA

insights.throughput: Throughput shows variability across 4 completion weeks. Recent uptick with 2 items completed week ending 12 Apr. Total of 6 items completed over the period.
insights.cycleTime: Cycle time varies significantly. Bug #1170800 took 23 days in active development (outlier). Recent items completing faster: 2-7 days cycle time. Average 8.0 days. Consider investigating why some items take much longer.
insights.leadTime: Lead time averages 24.8 days (median 21 days) from creation to completion. Much longer than cycle time, indicating items wait significantly in backlog before work begins. Longest: Bug #1170800 at 45 days total.
insights.workStartEfficiency: Average work start efficiency 35% - items spend only about 1/3 of total time in the workflow after leaving backlog. The remaining 65% is backlog delay before work starts. Best: Bug #1191895 at 87% (very quick from backlog to workflow). Worst: Items spending weeks in New before Ready for Dev. Improving this metric means better prioritization and faster movement from backlog to active workflow.
insights.cycleTimeFlowEfficiency: Average cycle time flow efficiency 62.5% - during workflow execution, about 62% of time is active work and 38% is waiting between stages. This measures handoff efficiency. Items spending significant time in Ready states (Ready for Dev, Ready for QA, Ready for Release) lower this metric. Best performers flow smoothly through stages. Focus: Reduce Ready/waiting states, improve team coordination.
insights.leadTimeFlowEfficiency: Average lead time flow efficiency 21.9% - overall, only about 22% of total time (from creation to done) is spent actively working. This combines both backlog delay (65% from Work Start Efficiency) and workflow waiting (38% from Cycle Time Flow Efficiency). While concerning, this is typical for many teams. Goal: Increase to >40% by reducing both backlog delay and workflow waiting time.
insights.cfd: System is significantly unstable. Arrival rate (5.6/week) far exceeds departure rate (2.0/week), causing backlog to grow by ~3.6 items/week. The gap between cumulative arrivals and departures is widening, indicating accumulating backlog. Trend lines show arrivals accelerating while departures remain relatively flat.
insights.workItemAge: Work items aging significantly in External Review (48 days max) and Ready for Dev (22 days). Most concerning: #1147602 (Bag - Badges) at 48 days in External Review, #1180335 (BDD flexibleSalesChannel) at 24 days in QA. Items waiting in Ready for Dev suggest intake/prioritization bottleneck. Focus on clearing older items before starting new work.
insights.dailyWip: WIP trending upward from 6 to 9-12 items over the period. Trend line shows consistent growth. Consider implementing WIP limits to prevent further accumulation and maintain sustainable delivery pace.
insights.staleWork: 5 items haven't been updated in 3-6 days. Most critical: #1148527 (Server Side view_item_list) at 6 days, #1147602 (Bag Badges) and #1180335 (BDD flexibleSalesChannel) at 5 days. Daily standup should focus on unblocking these items to prevent further aging.
insights.timeInColumn: Most time spent in QA (108 days) and External Review (72 days), indicating significant bottlenecks in review processes. In Development (22 days) and In Review (20 days) are more efficient. Consider increasing QA capacity or implementing automated testing to reduce time in QA column. External Review bottleneck suggests need for better coordination with external stakeholders.
insights.wipAgeBreakdown: Growing proportion of aged work (>14 days) from 2 to 4-6 items. Concerning pattern of work aging rather than flowing. Fresh work (â‰¤1 day) remains constant at 1 item, suggesting slow progression through workflow. Focus on completing older items.
insights.wip: 20 items currently in progress across all workflow stages. Items aging significantly in External Review (48 days max) and Ready for Dev (22 days). Average age 18.4 days. Consider WIP limits and completing existing work before starting new items.
insights.bugRate: **Completed work:** 2 bugs and 4 features delivered over the period (33.3% bug rate). Most recent week: all features, no bugs. Quality improving in recent completions.
insights.netFlow: Net flow volatile with significant swings. Worst week: -8 (14 Mar) when 13 items started but only 5 finished. Best week: +7 (4 Apr) when 7 items finished and 0 started. Recent trend balancing. Goal: Maintain near-zero by limiting new work starts.
insights.state: 112 items (85%) remain in New state. 20 items (15%) actively in progress across workflow. 2 items waiting in Ready for Dev. Large New backlog suggests need for prioritisation and grooming to feed the pipeline.
insights.blockedItems: Blocked items trending upward from 8 to peak of 15 (25 Apr), now stabilizing around 10-11 items. Trend indicates persistent blocking issues that need systematic resolution. Focus on identifying and removing common blockers.
insights.transitionRates: All transitions show arrival rates exceeding departure rates (ratios >1.0), indicating work is building up at each stage. Most critical bottlenecks: New â†’ Ready for Dev (1.33 ratio) and Ready for Dev â†’ In Development (1.31 ratio) show items piling up before development starts. Mid-workflow transitions also accumulating (1.05-1.15 ratios). Focus on increasing development capacity and grooming to pull more items from New.
